title: Snapshot
description: A content-addressed record of one raw API response, per D15. The record shape is exactly {source, request_params, response_body, response_hash, retrieved_at}; those five fields are required and everything else is optional context. Snapshots are the substrate of determinism: identical inputs under (1) a snapshot, (2) a configuration, and (3) a parser version produce byte-identical --json output, and determinism is never claimed for live calls. Eval snapshots live in core/tests/snapshots/ and are the only thing offline tests and benchmark runs read; the runtime cache in the platformdirs user cache dir is a separate store and never feeds evals. Replay of a missing snapshot fails loudly rather than silently going live.

source.description: Connector that produced the response. Known values: openalex, crossref, datacite, semantic_scholar, arxiv, pubmed, unpaywall, opencitations.

request_params.description: Canonicalized request parameters (keys sorted, values normalized). Together with source and endpoint these form the request key the store is looked up by. Polite-pool identifiers such as mailto values are normalized out so a snapshot does not depend on whose email made the call.

response_body.description: The raw response body as returned by the source: a decoded JSON value for JSON APIs, or a string for text formats such as the arXiv Atom feed. Stored verbatim so a re-parse under a new parser version replays from the same bytes.

response_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonicalized response body, hex encoded, optionally prefixed with 'sha256:'. This is the content address: it identifies the snapshot, joins it to record provenance (custom.source_response_hashes) and to provenance events (source_response_hashes), and is what `snapshot diff` compares.

retrieved_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the live retrieval that produced this snapshot. Recorded for staleness and drift reporting; it is NOT an input to any hash, so it can never perturb replay determinism.

endpoint.description: The endpoint path or full URL that was called, for example '/works/10.1038/nature14539'.

request_key.description: Store lookup key derived from (source, endpoint, canonicalized request_params).

http_status.description: HTTP status code of the recorded response. A recorded 404 is a legitimate snapshot: it is what makes a clean negative replayable.

media_type.description: Response media type, for example 'application/json' or 'application/atom+xml'.
